data.title: Markets extend morning gains, Sensex surges over 500 pts, led by PSU banks; mid, smallcaps shine
data.description:
Adani Ports, Adani Enterprises, Shriram Finance, UltraTech Cement, and Cipla were the top gainers on the Nifty.


Benchmark indices Nifty and Sensex extended morning gains driven by strength in public sector banks ahead of the RBI MPC meet outcome on Friday. Heavyweight stocks like HDFC Bank and Reliance Industries also contributed to the rally on December 3. Broader markets also showed strength with mid- and small-cap indices rising 0.7 percent each.

At noon, the Sensex was up 538.04 points or 0.67 percent at 80,786.12, and the Nifty was up 153.45 points or 0.63 percent at 24,429.50. About 2,404 shares advanced, 1,019 shares declined, and 86 shares unchanged.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

"The underlying resilience of the market is evident in its ability to bounce back. The market is not focusing on GDP growth slowdown but on the likely policy response to this slowdown. The rise in bank stocks indicates that the market is expecting a CRR cut on Friday, which will boost the profitability," V K Vijayakumar, Chief Market Strategist at Geojit Financial Services, said. He added that the news of higher GST proposals on products like tobacco can impact stocks like ITC and tax reductions on insurance premiums can be beneficial to insurance stocks.

Index heavyweight HDFC Bank rose over a percent after a significant block deal involving 21.7 lakh shares was executed, taking India’s largest private sector bank’s market capitalisation back above Rs 14 lakh crore. Reliance Industries which has gained 3 percent in the last two sessions, added 1 percent in the afternoon. Analysts suggest that recent corrections in large cap stocks offer safety in the current scenario.

The Nifty PSU Bank Index led sectoral gains, with SBI, the largest state-owned lender, climbing nearly 2 percent, while Canara Bank and Bank of Baroda also advanced. Metal stocks followed suit, driven by gains in Adani Enterprises, JSW Steel, and Vedanta, as experts anticipate that upcoming government infrastructure spending will boost demand in the sector over the next few quarters.

Also read: Speculation over RBI chief's future heats up after GDP miss

In contrast, FMCG stocks declined, with ITC and Varun Beverages under pressure amid concerns over GST rate rationalisation proposals for aerated drinks, cigarettes, and tobacco products.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The mid-small cap indices outperformed the frontline indices with gains of 0.7 and 0.6 percent, respectively. Market experts suggest that BSE Small-Cap and Mid-Cap have seen a decent correction in the past two months and could likely see renewed buying interest in the coming weeks.

Health insurance stocks surged up to 11 percent on December 3 after the Group of Ministers (GoM) on GST rate rationalisation proposed key relief measures for the sector. The panel, led by Bihar Deputy Chief Minister Samrat Chaudhary, recommended reducing GST on health insurance premiums from 18 percent to 5 percent. Additionally, it suggested exempting GST entirely on premiums for senior citizens.

Two significant block deals were executed in the afternoon, involving TBO Tek and Zen Technologies. Zen Technologies saw its shares decline over 7 percent following a Rs 386.1-crore block deal on the bourses. Similarly, TBO Tek fell 7 percent after 64.34 lakh shares, representing a 5.92 percent equity stake, changed hands at Rs 1,476 per share.

Swiggy shares trimmed early gains, trading 3 percent higher after rising 10 percent in the morning, as investors await its second-quarter earnings later today. This marks the company’s first earnings report as a listed entity following its Rs 11,327-crore IPO last month.

"Nifty continued its pullback, closing above 24,200, with broader market participation hinting at further upside. A decisive break above the 50-EMA at 24,400 could confirm the next leg up. Sensex extended gains from its recent low near 79,000 but needs to clear the 80,500 resistance for a breakout. Key support levels are at 79,800 for Sensex and 24,100 for Nifty, with resistance at 80,700 and 24,450, respectively," Vaishali Parekh, Vice President of Technical Research at Prabhudas Lilladher, said.

Adani Ports, Adani Enterprises, Shriram Finance, UltraTech Cement, and Cipla were the top gainers on the Nifty. ITC, Trent, Bharti Airtel, HDFC Life, and Tata Consumer Products were the key laggards.

data.title: Adani Ports extends gain to third day, top Nifty gainer after healthy business update
data.description:
The recovery follows a steep 13 percent decline between November 21 and 28

Shares of Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone (APSEZ) surged over 7 percent to Rs 1,310 per share on December 3 after the company came out with its strong business update till November this year. The optimism also built after CARE Ratings upgraded its recent acquisition - Gopalpur Ports to AA/Stable from BBB Rating Watch Positive.

This marked the third consecutive trading session of gains for the stock, with an 11 percent rally over the period. The recovery follows a steep 13 percent decline between November 21 and 28, driven by market reactions to bribery and other allegations involving Gautam Adani and senior executives of the Adani Group.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Catch all the market action on our LIVE blog

For the period from April to November 2024, APSEZ reported handling 293.7 MMT of cargo, registering a year-on-year growth of 7 percent. This performance was bolstered by a sharp 19 percent rise in container volumes and a 7 percent increase in liquids and gas volumes.

Despite challenges such as a worker strike at Gangavaram Port and adverse weather conditions at Mudra and Tuna, the management reaffirmed its FY25 cargo volume guidance of 460-480 MMT and projected revenue of approximately Rs 30,000 crore.

In addition, the company highlighted that its logistics rail volumes grew by 10 percent year-on-year to 0.42 million TEUs, while GPWIS volumes increased by 15 percent to 14.2 MMT.

Recently, CARE Ratings upgraded Gopalpur Port's credit rating by six notches, from BBB (Rating Watch Positive) to AA/Stable, attributing the improvement to the strong parentage of APSEZ, India’s largest port operator, which manages 10 ports and three terminals and handles 27 percent of the nation’s seaborne cargo.

However, rating strengths are tempered by cargo and client concentration risks besides evacuation challenges reflected from operational performance of the first half of the current fiscal.

Story continues below Advertisement

data.title: Why SEBI is trying to avoid listing of clearing corporations by proxy
data.description:
Clearing corporations (CCs) are not allowed to be listed and the regulator wants to ensure that they are not listed by proxy when their parent exchanges get listed.

The capital markets regulator has proposed that the ownership of clearing corporations (CCs) be diversified.

Currently, the majority holding or 51 percentage of the holding of CCs has to be with a stock exchange or stock exchanges. In a consultation paper issued on November 22, the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) suggested an overhaul.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Legal experts told Moneycontrol that these changes have been proposed to ensure that the CCs stay focused on their core responsibility of maintaining market stability and not be diverted from this for pursuing their shareholders' commercial interests.

Therefore, CCs are not allowed to be listed and the regulator wants to ensure that they are not listed by proxy when their parent exchanges get listed. As the regulator's consultation paper said, "When the exchange itself is listed, this leads to the vicarious listing of its subsidiary CC".

Also read: NSE, BSE will act as alternative trading venues for each other from April 1, 2025, says SEBI

Abhiraj Arora, Partner at Saraf and Partners, said: "Clearing corporations are crucial entities in the financial market, responsible for managing risks and ensuring the smooth settlement of trades. The reason they are not allowed to be listed on stock exchanges is to prevent commercial pressures from influencing their operations."

"Listing could expose CCs to shareholder demands and market fluctuations, which might conflict with their primary role of maintaining market stability and protecting public interest," he said.

Listing, or vicarious listing, could also subject the CCs to market fluctuations, Natasha Treasurywala, Partner at Desai & Diwanji, pointed out.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Treasurywala said that the listing could create a conflict of interest between the CCs' role as an intermediary and their responsibility towards their public shareholders.

"The concern is that they may prioritise profits over their business," she said. "There is also a systemic risk in the listing of CCs. If CCs are subject to market fluctuations, it could compromise their ability to fulfil their systemic responsibilities. This is also in line with the laws of certain other jurisdictions," she added.

One of the leading exchanges, the National Stock Exchange (NSE), has been making attempts to list and Treasurywala believes that the these plans definitely have a bearing on the regulator's plans on diversifying CCs' ownership. NSE owns NSE Clearing.

According to Arora, the potential listing of the exchange could have partly influenced the regulator's proposal to diversify CCs' ownership.

"When an exchange is listed, there is a risk that shareholder interests might overshadow regulatory concerns," he said.

Regulatory hurdle

Currently, since CCs have to be majority owned by stock exchanges, they are excluded from the Payment and Settlement Systems (PSS) Act. The PSS Act falls under the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI's) ambit.

But if a CC's ownership is going to be so diversified that a stock exchange or stock exchanges do not hold majority shareholding, there is a concern that the CCs may be brought under this Act.

The markets regulator is in talks with the RBI to seek clarity on whether CCs can continue to be excluded from the Act, even with the new structure of ownership.

This is necessary to ensure that there is no regulatory overlap, according to Smriti Nair, Partner at Juris Corp Advocates and Solicitors.

Nair said, "Currently one of the main functions of such CCs is to clear and settle trades for recognised stock exchanges. Further, any dispute arising out of such clearing and settlement service is brought under the arbitration mechanism as provided under stock exchanges. This gives SEBI  more regulatory powers to monitor the activities of the CCs."

She added, "Including such CCs under the PSS Act and bringing them under the purview of th RBI (which is the relevant regulator for PSS Act) may create a regulatory overlap."
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Recent correction cools down Nifty FY26e P/E to 19.5x, DIIs buy shares worth $58.9 billion YTD
data.description:
Nifty 50 index is currently trading at a P/E multiple of 19.5x FY26e EPS, however the broader market indices are still hovering at high valuations of 30x and 24x for mid and smallcap indices.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

The recent stock market correction has cooled off valuation for largecap but the broader market indices are still trading at expensive multiples, a Motilal Oswal note has said on December 3.

Accordingly, MOSL said its Model Portfolio is Overweight on 'domestic structural as well as cyclical themes' such as Consumer Discretionary, Industrials, and Real Estate, along with IT, Healthcare and BFSI. The house is Underweight on Metals, Energy and Automobiles.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Between the October-November period, FIIs sold shares worth $13 billion, cooling off stock valuations in largecaps. Nifty 50 index is currently trading at a P/E multiple of 19.5x FY26e EPS, however the broader market indices are still hovering at high valuations of 30x and 24x for mid and smallcap indices.

Despite the second month of fall, Nifty 50's YTD performance shows a rise of 11% so far, with small and midcaps outperforming the largecap indices. This was also the second month of selling by foreign investors, pulling out $2.2 billion while DIIs have ploughed in $5.3 billion in equities in November. For the year so far, FIIs have sold shares worth $2.1 billion as against a net buy figure in 2023. DII bought equities worth $58.9 billion so far this year, after putting in $22.3 billion in 2023.

Benchmark indices have corrected by 8% in the third quarter, have tested a technical correction in between, citing earnings slowdown, high valuations, geopolitical tensions and the rise of the US Dollar after Donald Trump's victory in the US elections.

The year seems to be wrapping up with India equities outperforming MSCI EM index, but behind China and US stock markets, both in USD terms as well as in local currency.

The year so far has also seen India's overall market capitalisation rise by 36.6%, the largest growth for any major market, even ahead of US' 32% m-cap growth.

MOSL said among the top names in its model portfolios include largecaps like ICICI Bank, Bharti Airtel, SBI, HCL Technologies, L&T, M&M, Power Grid, Titan, Trent, and Mankind Pharma. Among mid and smallcaps, it shared names like Indian Hotels, Cummins India, Persistent Systems, Dixon Tech, Godrej Properties, Coforge, Metro Brands, Global Health, Angel One, PNB Housing, and Cello World.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

data.title: NTPC Green Energy shares extend post-listing gains, hit 10% upper circuit amid high volumes
data.description:
NTPC Green Energy shares hit an all-time high on December 3.

  • Watchlist

Volume
Todays L/H

NTPC Green Energy shares hit an all-time high in December 3 trade after the stock was locked in the upper circuit amid high volumes.

NTPC Green Energy stock hit the 10 percent upper circuit to quote at Rs 142.12 per share on the NSE. The stock was listed on the exchanges on November 27 at a premium of over 3 percent.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The scrip has been gaining for the last two days and has risen 13.84 percent in the period.

NTPC Green Energy shares were listed at Rs 111.50 per share on the NSE, a premium of 3.24 percent over the IPO price of Rs 108 per share.

On the BSE, the NTPC Green Energy stock was listed at Rs 111.60 apiece at a premium of 3.33 percent.

data.title: IPO frenzy cools down in November as subscription levels take a hit
data.description:
In November, while seven companies went public, the larger ones were Swiggy with an issue size of Rs 11,327 crore and NTPC Green Energy’s IPO totaling Rs 10,000 crore.

Demand in the primary market remained largely subdued in the month of November as average subscription levels, both in the mainboard and SME IPO segments, witnessed a significant decline when compared from the earlier months.

Data from Prime Database shows that the average subscription level for mainboard IPOs in November fell to 1.9 times, a sharp decline from 16 times in October and 76 times in September.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Incidentally, the IPO of Enviro Infra Engineers, which was subscribed 221 times, was excluded from the calculation to avoid skewing the data as excluding Enviro, all the IPOs that were launched in November saw subscription levels in a modest range of 1.5 times to 2.4 times.

In November, while seven companies went public, the larger ones were Swiggy with an issue size of Rs 11,327 crore and the Rs 10,000-crore NTPC Green Energy IPO.

"Over the last month, markets have seen volatility because of geopolitical concerns and persistent FII selling that has dampened the sentiment thereby affecting primary markets too which saw lower subscriptions lately," said Prashant Rao, director of equity capital markets at Anand Rathi Investment Banking. He added that even though the euphoria has come down a bit, the pipeline still remains strong and quality issuances will still see reasonable subscriptions.

Among mainboard IPOs that saw the maximum subscription, food delivery app Swiggy led the pack with a subscription of 2.4 times, followed by Sagility India at 2.2 times.

In October and November, majority of public issues were large in size and belonged to companies with scaled up businesses, said Bhavesh Shah, managing director and head of investment banking at Equirus. He said that typically, when a company with a large issue size or significant scale goes for an IPO, its growth potential needs to be weighed with the valuations compared to smaller, high-growth firms.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

In 2024, among the mainboard IPOs, Hyundai Motors India was the biggest in terms of issue size of Rs 27,855 crore. It was followed by Swiggy and NTPC Green Energy.

SME IPOs see a similar decline in subscription levels

The situation is similar for SME IPOs as well. The average subscription in the SME segment in November fell to 112 times, a decline from 242 times in September.

In the SME segment, the IPO of Rajputana Biodiesel was the most subscribed in November at 506 times. It was followed by Onynx Biotech, which was subscribed 138 times. So far this calendar year, 75 mainboard and 225 SME IPOs have come to the market.

In the current calendar year, Danish Power was the biggest in terms of issue size. It had an issue size of Rs 188 crore. It was followed by K P Green Engineering and Sahasra Electronics Solutions with an issue size of Rs 180 crore and Rs 176 crore, respectively.

The last few months have seen regulatory tightening and enhanced monitoring of the SME IPO segment, which has also affected the subscription and listing trends of the segment.

data.title: Niva Bupa, Star Health shares rise up to 11% amid likely GST reduction on health insurance premia
data.description:
Niva Bupa, Star Health shares rise up to 11% amid likely GST reduction on health insurance premia

Health insurance stocks rose up to 11% on December 3 as the GoM on GST rate rationalisation has proposed a flurry of measures for the sector.

The Group of Ministers (GoM) on rate rationalisation under Bihar Deputy Chief Minister Samrat Chaudhary proposed to reduce GST from 18% to 5% on health insurance premia.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The GoM also proposed to exempt GST on health insurance premia for senior citizens from current 18%.

The GoM also proposed to exempt GST on health insurance premia for non-senior citizens up to a cover of Rs 5 lakh per annum.

The GST Council will meet in Jaisalmer on Dec 21 to also take up the proposal of GST on life and health insurance premiums.

The GoM also proposed to exempt GST on pure term life insurance premia from current 18%.

The proposals led to a positive move for the health insurance stocks with Niva Bupa rising over 11% on December 3 while Star Health rose over 2%.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: MapMyIndia's Rohan Verma says will run new consumer business with 'own funds', not taking CE Info's money
data.description:
MapMyIndia also clarified that the company has an option to invest Rs 35 crore through CCDs as an additional fund into the new consumer company.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

The management of CE Info Systems, the parent entity of MapMyIndia brand has said that they will not be utilising any of the company's funds for the new consumer business that has been set up, and instead, the hived off B2C segment will be run using the promoter's 'own funds'.

In conversation with CNBC-TV18 on December 3, MapMyIndia clarified that the company has an option to invest Rs 35 crore through CCDs as additional funds into the new consumer company.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Regarding the 10% equity in the new entity, the management said MapMyIndia is getting 10% share in the new consumer business only for Rs 10 lakh, and both companies will be able to use the Maapls brands.

Rakesh Verma, co-founder and CMD, CE Info Systems added the brand Mappls will be used by both B2C as well as B2B entities for five years at no additional royalty.

Shares of the map company have been under selling pressure after the management's decision to hive off the consumer business to be run by the founder's son, who will step down from executive role by April next year. Several market participants criticised the decision raising questions whether the decision was 'shortchanging' the shareholders.

Shares of CE Info Systems continue to face selling pressure, lower by over 6% as investors continue remain apprehensive about the business impact of the decision to hive off the consumer  business.

Co-founder and CMD Rakesh Verma added that CE Info Systems' B2C business will still have an annual cash burn rate of Rs 30 crore, which will continue to increase.

This is being updated
